rollback_reason: The failed cutover left the replacement lane in a state that was less explainable than the known-good OAI reference lane.

pre_rollback_state.observed_problem: The lane could not keep the required NGAP subset explicit beyond Uplink NAS Transport while proving attach-plus-ping after cutover.

post_rollback_state.repair_state: reference lane restored and the required NGAP subset is again explicit through UE Context Release

procedures.name: NG Setup
procedures.detail: the declared Open5GS endpoint still accepts NG Setup after cutover

procedures.name: Initial UE Message
procedures.detail: initial UE access still reaches the declared NGAP lane after cutover

procedures.name: Uplink NAS Transport
procedures.detail: uplink NAS transport still reaches the real core after cutover

procedures.name: Downlink NAS Transport
procedures.detail: downlink NAS transport is no longer deterministic enough to trust the cutover lane

procedures.name: UE Context Release
procedures.detail: cleanup evidence is deferred until rollback review resolves the lane state

procedures.name: NG Setup
procedures.detail: the rollback target accepted NG Setup after restoration

procedures.name: Initial UE Message
procedures.detail: initial UE access is again accepted on the rollback target

procedures.name: Uplink NAS Transport
procedures.detail: uplink NAS transport reaches the rollback target core lane

procedures.name: Downlink NAS Transport
procedures.detail: downlink NAS transport again returns registration progress toward the UE

procedures.name: UE Context Release
procedures.detail: cleanup and release state are again auditable on the rollback target

operator_notes: Rollback was preferred because leaving the lane up would hide that the required NGAP subset was no longer explicit beyond Uplink NAS Transport. The rollback target is explicit and the restored state is auditable through UE Context Release.
